title: reactions/ handler silently defaults to emoji='?' for malformed value
description: In message_set update, reactions/ handler does value.get("emoji").and_then(|v| v.as_str()).unwrap_or("?") — a non-object value (e.g. a string) silently becomes emoji='?' and sentAt=''. Now that known fields (body/readAt/deletedAt) validate types strictly, a contributor adding reaction validation will see two inconsistent patterns and may copy the permissive one. Inconsistency generates future bug reports when reactions with garbled emoji appear.
close_reason: Fixed: reaction value is now validated to be null or object; scalar values return invalidArguments instead of creating garbled emoji='?' reaction
